name: GLM-5.2 (Max)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DSA + 'IndexShare' sparse-attention indexer reuse (2.9x fewer per-token FLOPs at 1M context), 1M-token context; selectable High/Max reasoning-effort modes
note: Highest-capability reasoning-effort tier: raises the thinking-token budget (~85k tokens on hard tasks vs High's ~50% of that) for complex, long-horizon agentic problems. Same weights as the High tier.

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools; with-tools variant reaches 54.7
SWE-bench Pro; matches figure independently repeated on the HF blog
Terminal-Bench 2.1 (Terminus-2 harness), vs Claude Opus 4.8's 85.0 in the same table; a 'best reported' config reaches 82.7 per the HF blog
Artificial Analysis's independent IFBench measurement for GLM-5.2 (max), surfaced via the BenchLM.ai AA-data mirror. Not reported by Zhipu/Z.ai itself.
Artificial Analysis's own GDPval-AA v2 re-evaluation of GLM-5.2 (max) (Elo-like 0-2000 scale), from AA's dedicated launch-analysis article. Independent of Zhipu's self-reported numbers.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Claude Code harness, Max reasoning effort: Pass rate 20.4% / Score 40.6% (rank 25). The leaderboard's 'Max' effort label matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel field ('Max', isBestConfig=true) confirming the correct variant match. Score component used per corpus convention.

Artificial Analysis's own Tau2-Bench Telecom leaderboard entry for GLM-5.2 (max): 99.1%, directly confirmed by fetching AA's public evaluation leaderboard page ('GLM-5.2 (max) with a score of 99.1%'), also mirrored in BenchLM.ai's embedded model data. Independent of Zhipu's self-reported figures and not currently tracked for this model (sibling releases GLM-4.5-Air and GLM-4.6 have self-/independently-reported Tau2-Telecom scores, but GLM-5.2 (Max) did not until now).

MCP-Atlas score from the official GLM-5.2 launch blog's benchmark table. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Verified via the page's embedded JSON payload for model id zai/glm-5.2 (accuracy 69.5, stderr 1.179, reasoning_effort='max'), not just the rendered bar.

Page titled 'SWE-bench Verified'. Verified via the page's embedded JSON payload for model id zai/glm-5.2 (accuracy 82.8, stderr 1.689, reasoning_effort='max'). Distinct from the existing self-reported SWE-bench Pro (62.1) already in this file under the separate 'swe-bench-pro' slug.

FrontierBench v0.1 (74 tasks, 7 domains), Claude Code harness, Max reasoning-effort tier: resolution rate 5.1% ±1.0%, rank 8 of 8 on the official leaderboard; 3.8B tokens, ~$4.2k run cost. Run by the FrontierBench team (Terminal-Bench/Harbor maintainers). Leaderboard lists the model as 'GLM 5.2 (max)' — mapped to the corpus's glm-5-2-max tier row. Only open-weights model on the v0.1 leaderboard.

verificationNotes:
See GLM-5.2 (High) entry for shared model-level verification (existence, dates, params, license). This entry carries the publicly reported self-reported benchmark scores, on the assumption (not fully confirmed by Z.ai) that the published numbers reflect the top/Max reasoning-effort configuration -- Z.ai did not publish an explicit per-tier breakdown at launch per MarkTechPost's coverage. Scores cross-checked between huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog (HLE, AIME, GPQA, and an extended coding-benchmark table) and official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2 (Terminal-Bench 2.1 81.0 vs Claude Opus 4.8's 85.0, SWE-bench Pro 62.1, FrontierSWE within 1% of Opus 4.8) -- both agree on SWE-bench Pro 62.1 and Terminal-Bench 2.1 ~81.0, giving good confidence in those two figures. Omitted CritPt (16.7), HMMT Nov 2025 (94.4), HMMT Feb 2026 (92.5), IMOAnswerBench (91.0), NL2Repo (48.9), DeepSWE (46.2), ProgramBench (63.7), FrontierSWE (74.4), PostTrainBench (34.3), SWE-Marathon (13.0), MCP-Atlas (76.8), and Tool-Decathlon (48.2) for lack of a matching corpus benchmark slug.

[2026-07-16 gap-closure pass] Resolved all 6 flagged core-benchmark gaps for GLM-5.2 (Max). ADDED three new results: LiveCodeBench 69.5% and SWE-bench (Verified) 82.8%, both independently measured by Vals AI for model id zai/glm-5.2 with reasoning_effort='max' (confirmed via the site's embedded JSON payload, not just the animated score bar -- www.vals.ai/benchmarks/lcb and www.vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench); and a second independent SWE-bench (Verified) reading of 78.7% from Epoch AI's raw results feed (epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv, model id 'glm-5.2_max', run 2026-06-25) -- this Epoch row had already surfaced in an earlier research round while checking the GLM-5.2 (High) sibling but was correctly withheld there (wrong tier) and is only now attached to its correct (Max) home. OMITTED four core benchmarks after exhausting all three required source types: (1) official launch materials -- huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog and docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2 were directly re-fetched and neither publishes MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, HumanEval, or MMMU for this model, only the same benchmark set already in this file; (2) independent leaderboards -- Artificial Analysis's current Intelligence Index v4.1 composite (GDPval-AA v2, tau2/tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR) excludes all four as legacy/saturated; Vals AI's full tracked benchmark set for zai/glm-5.2 (20 benchmarks, enumerated directly from the page's link list) contains MMLU Pro, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, GPQA, Terminal-Bench, ProgramBench etc. but no plain MMLU, MATH-500, HumanEval, or MMMU; Epoch AI's raw benchmarks.csv feed has exactly 8 rows for model id 'glm-5.2_max' (EBR-bench, SWE-Bench verified, OTIS Mock AIME, GPQA diamond, FrontierMath tiers 1-3 and 4, SimpleQA Verified, Chess Puzzles) and none of the four; llm-stats.com's GLM-5.2 page was checked and confirmed to show no numeric scores for any benchmark, only navigation mentions; (3) sibling cross-reference -- the GLM-5.1 predecessor and the GLM-5.2 (High) sibling entries in this corpus were both re-read and neither has MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/MMMU results either, and GLM-5.2 (High)'s own verificationNotes document a prior round's targeted OpenCompass/SuperCLUE check (2026-07-15) that likewise found zero GLM-5.2 entries on those regional trackers. MMMU is additionally structurally inapplicable: this corpus's own capabilities block for GLM-5.2 (Max) declares vision=false and modalities=["text"] -- MMMU is a multimodal-image benchmark that a text-only model was never going to be run on. This is consistent with the corpus's own benchmark-catalog framing of MMLU ("largely saturated at the frontier") and HumanEval ("long-saturated at the frontier") as legacy evaluations frontier labs increasingly skip in favor of harder successors (MMLU-Pro, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified/Pro) -- exactly the substitutions observed here. No existing entries were altered.

[2026-07-17 ROUND-22 second-pass gap chase for mmlu/math/humaneval, new source types only] Per instructions, deliberately did NOT re-check official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, or BenchLM.ai (already exhausted in the 2026-07-16 gap-closure pass documented above). Checked all 6 newly-specified source types; all six came back negative for mmlu/math/humaneval, with no credible number found anywhere.

(1) Stanford HELM: crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest/ (and /lite/, /capabilities/) fetched directly — pages are JS-rendered and returned no table data to a plain fetch, consistent with every prior HELM check in this corpus. Decisive structural finding via search: HELM entered maintenance mode on 2026-06-01 (per its own docs), i.e. 12 days *before* GLM-5.2's 2026-06-13 release — HELM stopped onboarding new models before this model existed, so it could not have evaluated it under any circumstance. Source type exhausted with a structural (not just search-failure) negative.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: WebSearched "GLM-5.2 MMLU/MATH/HumanEval site:arxiv.org" repeatedly — only hit is the GLM-5 team's own paper (arXiv:2602.15763, "GLM-5: from Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering"), which predates GLM-5.2 by four months and cannot contain its scores. Per the sibling GLM-5.json entry's own prior research (already in this corpus), that paper's Table 11 MMLU(EM)=88.3/MATH(EM)=56.4 apply only to "GLM-5-Base," a distinct pre-RL pretrained checkpoint, not even the released GLM-5 model, let alone GLM-5.2 — irrelevant here even if it did mention 5.2. GLM-5.2 has no dedicated arXiv paper of its own. Additionally leveraged the sibling GLM-5.2 (High) entry's Round-20 (2026-07-16) exhaustive Semantic Scholar bulk-search (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search/bulk, 122 hits reviewed) which found zero third-party papers citing GLM-5.2 in any benchmark comparison table — directly applicable evidence since High and Max share the same underlying released model/HF repo. Negative.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs: found one actually-committed benchmark report for this exact model — github.com/przeprogramowani/10x-bench/blob/master/glm-5.2-report.md — fetched directly; it uses a custom proprietary "10xBench" app-building rubric (5 generation attempts, 10-point manual scoring rubric, unrelated to MMLU/MATH/HumanEval), reporting only an aggregate 8.5/10, not a standard-benchmark score. Searched further for lm-evaluation-harness/results.json runs against "zai/glm-5.2" or "glm-5.2" — found only vLLM/ROCm deployment recipes (inference-serving guides, no eval output) and one WebSearch synthesis mentioning generic "lm_eval --tasks gsm8k" usage with no linked repo or results file, too generic/uncitable to use. Negative.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's model page (openrouter.ai/z-ai/glm-5.2) lists only Zhipu's existing self-reported set (SWE-bench Pro 62.1, Terminal-Bench 82.7, MCP-Atlas 76.8, Tool-Decathlon 48.2, GPQA 91.2, AIME 99.2, HLE 40.5) — no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. llm-stats.com's dedicated MATH-500 leaderboard (llm-stats.com/benchmarks/math-500) fetched directly: 32 models listed, only "GLM-4.5" present (rank 3, 0.982) — no GLM-5.2 variant at all. LiveBench.ai: search confirms GLM-5.2 IS tracked there (May 2026 snapshot: 78.57 Coding Avg, 58.33 Agentic Coding Avg) but these are LiveBench's own proprietary contamination-free composite categories, not the specific MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval benchmarks this corpus's benchmarks.json defines under the 'mmlu'/'math'/'humaneval' slugs — using them would be exactly the kind of variant-conflation error this corpus's own dataset audit flags as its top risk, so correctly not used. pricepertoken.com's MMLU and HumanEval leaderboard pages both returned HTTP 403 (blocked, not a clean negative — inconclusive/inaccessible). Scale AI SEAL and PapersWithCode: searched, no GLM-5.2 entries surfaced for any of these three benchmarks. Net: negative (with two inaccessible sites noted as inconclusive rather than confirmed-absent).

(5) Non-English-language sources: checked Chinese (Zhihu 知乎, SegmentFault, notes.kamacoder.com, attempted Baidu Baike — blocked/DNS-unreachable from this environment) and Japanese (Business Insider Japan, eigent.ai/ja, weel.co.jp, ai-souken.com) coverage. A specific claim ("MMLU approaching 85%", "HumanEval pass@1 78%") recurs across several low-quality SEO/blog articles, but every one directly fetched (segmentfault.com/a/1190000047898612, notes.kamacoder.com/llm/news/glm-5-2.html) explicitly attributes it to unnamed "公开评测/public evaluations" with zero traceable primary source, and one search synthesis explicitly flagged cross-source inconsistency (elsewhere the same articles cite HumanEval at 92.3% instead of 78%, and MBPP at 94.1%), recommending "official sources and Code Arena blind testing" as the only reliable reference instead. The kamacoder.com article additionally states outright that Z.ai's actual release-day materials published NO benchmark table at all ("官方发布当天，一张 benchmark 跑分表都没放"), directly undercutting the credibility of any specific-number claim appearing elsewhere. Per this task's explicit rule against inventing/estimating scores, these uncited, mutually-contradictory SEO-farm figures were treated as noise, not a source, and none were used. Negative.

(6) Sibling models: re-read glm-5.json, glm-5-1.json, and glm-5-2-high.json in full. None of the three report mmlu, math, or humaneval for themselves, and none contain a comparison table that includes a GLM-5.2 (Max) score for these benchmarks — consistent with (and reinforcing) each entry's own documented finding that this is a deliberate Z.ai policy starting at the GLM-5 generation (GLM-5's own verificationNotes note that predecessor GLM-4.5 DID publish MMLU=90/MATH-500=98.2, confirming the newer models' omission is an active choice, not an untested gap). Negative.

Conclusion: genuinely exhausted all 6 newly-specified source types (four fully negative, one — non-English blogs — surfaced only non-credible uncited/contradictory figures correctly rejected, one — LiveBench/pricepertoken — partially inconclusive due to site access limits and category mismatch). No credible, citable mmlu/math/humaneval score exists for GLM-5.2 (Max) from any of these avenues. All three remain omitted_no_evidence. No existing results were altered or added.

[2026-07-18 MMMU gap-chase] Chased the flagged 'mmmu' (MMMU) gap for GLM-5.2 (Max). This corpus's own modalities/capabilities block for this model already declares modalities=["text"] and capabilities.vision=false, i.e. GLM-5.2 (Max) is structurally text-only with no vision encoder -- MMMU (a multi-image VQA benchmark) is architecturally inapplicable, not merely unpublished. Rather than stop at that internal field, independently re-verified modality via two fresh external fetches specifically for this pass: (1) docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2, the official Z.ai docs, re-fetched directly -- explicitly states "input modalities: text only" and "output modalities: text only," with no mention of MMMU or any vision benchmark anywhere on the page; (2) apidog.com/blog/glm-5-2-benchmarks, an independent benchmark-roundup article, which states outright "Modality: Text in, text out (no confirmed vision variant)" and explicitly debunks a circulating rumor of a vision-capable "GLM-5.2V" variant: "there is no GLM-5.2 vision model... if you see 'GLM-5.2V' somewhere, it isn't a thing Z.ai has confirmed." Also ran web searches for "GLM-5.2 MMMU vision benchmark" and "GLM-5.2 vision-language multimodal Z.ai"; the only source claiming GLM-5.2 is multimodal/handles images was a single low-quality SEO blog (buildfastwithai.com), directly contradicted by both the official docs.z.ai page and the more detailed apidog.com writeup, and inconsistent with this corpus's own established capabilities.vision=false flag from prior verification rounds -- treated as an unreliable outlier, not credible evidence. No MMMU score for GLM-5.2 (Max) appears in any official launch material (huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog, docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2), independent tracker/blog (apidog.com, benchlm.ai, datacamp.com, mindstudio.ai, thezvi.substack.com), or sibling-model comparison table surveyed. Conclusion: MMMU is architecturally inapplicable for GLM-5.2 (Max) -- a text-only model was never going to be run on an image-based benchmark -- not a case of an unfound-but-existing score. Proposed omitted_no_evidence with reason "architecturally inapplicable (text-only model)." No existing results altered; no new result added for mmmu.

2026-07-24 FRONTIERBENCH: ingested the FrontierBench v0.1 leaderboard row (see the result's own notes for harness, effort, CI, tokens and cost).
